your_book:
Jaleel's book added a solid session on July 3, ending at $20,697.98 with an unrealized gain of $158.01 (+0.77%). The day's heavy lifting came from a broad healthcare and tech rally: ADBE surged +4.14% (still -10.4% from cost — that gap matters), ABT gained +3.49% and sits at a healthy +10.3%, ZTS added +3.24% but remains -5.0% in the hole, and BSX was the day's healthcare standout at +4.83% — yet it too sits -5.7% from cost with a guidance-slashed flag hanging over it. VRTX led the book at +6.03%, now +18.9% from cost — the cleanest win in the pharma cluster. IOT (Samsara) jumped +5.12% to a +9.2% gain, but an AVOID verdict and a QARP of 48.5 mean that move doesn't change the thesis question. Only NVDA closed red at -1.39%, trimming a still-positive +6.2% position gain.

The drift problem is concentrated in three names. ADBE — the single largest position at 16.6% and $3,442 — carries a STRONG BUY verdict and a 4.14% pop today, but is still -10.4% from cost. Price and thesis are running in opposite directions: the model loves it, the market doesn't. That's a real divergence to respect, not explain away. ZTS and BSX share the same pattern — STRONG BUY verdicts, solid QARP scores (84.6 and 81.7), meaningful day gains, yet both remain underwater from cost with active risk flags. LULU (-7.4% from cost, guidance cut, Americas comps -5% for five straight quarters) is the quietest drift: +1.74% today changed nothing structurally. RKLB remains the book's only AVOID at meaningful size ($1,306, 6.3% weight) and added just +0.39%.

Watch the BSX earnings call around July 23 closely — management already guided flat through Q3 after slashing FY26 growth from 10.5% to 7–8.5%, and at -5.7% from cost the position needs a credible re-acceleration signal, not just a beat-and-maintain. NOW reports around the same date; at 14.4% of the book it's the concentration risk in a rate-sensitive name. KO's unusual options activity (Jul 17 $83 calls sweep, 2,061 contracts vs. 362 OI) is worth noting ahead of July 28 earnings — small position but the signal is unusually directional.

attention.headline: Largest position, -10.4% from cost — thesis and price still diverging
attention.note: A 4.14% day is welcome, but at 16.6% of the book and -10.4% from cost, ADBE's STRONG BUY verdict hasn't been validated by price in any durable way; the AI-narrative overhang (Claude Design, CFO exit, ARR de-emphasis) is the real driver and the next catalyst isn't until ~Sep 11.

attention.headline: Guidance slashed, earnings ~Jul 23 — need re-acceleration or the thesis cracks
attention.note: BSX popped +4.83% today but is still -5.7% from cost with FY26 growth cut to 7–8.5% and management explicitly guiding flat through Q3; the July 23 print is a binary moment for whether the STRONG BUY verdict holds.

attention.headline: AVOID verdict, 6.3% weight, QARP 37.0 — position-sizing conflict unresolved
attention.note: Rocket Lab is the book's lowest-QARP holding by a wide margin and carries an AVOID verdict, yet it represents $1,306 and 6.3% of a capital-preservation portfolio — that misalignment deserves an explicit decision, not ongoing inertia.

attention.headline: Five straight quarters of negative Americas comps, guidance cut — drift accelerating
attention.note: Today's +1.74% does nothing to address the structural deterioration: Americas comps -5% for a fifth consecutive quarter, tariff margin pressure, and China stalled, with the next read not until ~Sep 4.
